content.problemStatement:
You are given an array arrarrarr consisting of only the integers 000, 111, and 222. Your task is to find the number of contiguous subarrays that contain at least one occurrence of each integer (0, 1, and 2).


Input Format

The first line contains one integer nnn, the size of the array.                                                                                                               - Next line contains nnn integers arr[0],arr[1]...arr[n]arr[0],arr[1]...arr[n]arr[0],arr[1]...arr[n], representing the elements of the array.


Output Format

Print the count of all subarrays that contains at least one occurrence of each integer (0,1,2)(0, 1, 
 2)(0,1,2).
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10^5), the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers each being 0, 1 or 2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of contiguous subarrays that contain at least one occurrence of each of the three values 0, 1 and 2.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The subarrays that contain at least one 0, 1 and 2 are: [0,1,2], [0,1,2,0] and [1,2,0].
No subarray contains all three values.

solution.approach: The required subarrays are those that contain all three values. Using a sliding window we maintain the smallest right index r for each left index l such that the window [l, r) already has at least one 0, one 1 and one 2. Once this condition is satisfied, any extension of the window to the right (i.e., any end index ≥ r) will also satisfy it, therefore for this l we can add (N - r + 1) to the answer. We then move l forward, decreasing the count of arr[l] and possibly shrinking the window. The whole process runs in O(N) per test case because both pointers only move forward.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(ΣN) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input array.
